response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack/web engineer with roughly 15 years of professional experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and backend product development (TypeScript, Node.js, D, PHP). The role requires SoC and IP modeling, GPU/accelerator architecture knowledge, and deep-learning workload performance analysis — none of which appear anywhere in his history. His closest relevant work (low-latency D services at Sociomantic, C/C++ IDE components at National Instruments, an audio-DSP thesis) is peripheral to hardware performance architecture. He meets the years-of-experience bar on paper but not the domain requirements for a principal-level hardware performance role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, backend, desktop, and embedded contexts
Some systems-level performance exposure: low-latency async I/O services in D handling millions of requests per second
C/C++ experience (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, wave field synthesis thesis) and strong testing/craftsmanship discipline
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or system-on-chip architecture experience whatsoever
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD performance-analysis background
Career trajectory is senior full-stack product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or deep-learning performance background
General engineering seniority and systems-level performance exposure
